sections.content: The board changed after pressing RIGHT. Let me check what exactly changed by comparing the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

# Find what changed - compare node positions
print("=== BEFORE ===")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\n=== AFTER ===")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing RIGHT:
1. The green square with W center (node 6) moved from cols 18-20 to cols 21-23 (shifted right by 3)
2. The green square with B center (node 7) changed to a white block (node 7 now color=w), and a new green square (node 8) appeared at cols 27-29
3. The white block (node 8) became a green square (node 8 now color=N)
4. The W center dot moved from (31,19) to (31,22)
5. The B center dot stayed at (31,28)
6. The bottom bar changed - it was 64 pixels of c, now it's 63 pixels of c + 1 pixel of W
So it seems like the green squares are moving right, and when they move, they leave behind a white block. The bottom bar seems to be a progress/timer bar that's changing.
Let me think about this:
- There are two "agents" - the green squares
- The one with W center moved right, the one with B center seems to have moved too
- The bottom bar is changing (64 c -> 63 c + 1 W)
Wait, looking more carefully:
- Before: node 6 (N, green) at 18-20, node 7 (N, green) at 27-29, node 8 (w, white) at 30-32
- After: node 6 (N, green) at 21-23, node 7 (w, white) at 24-26, node 8 (N, green) at 27-29, node 9 (w, white) at 30-32
So the pattern is: both green squares moved right by 3 cells. The old position of the first green square became white, and the old white block stayed white.
Actually wait - the green squares seem to be moving one at a time. Let me reconsider:
- The left green square (with W center) moved from 18-20 to 21-23 (right by 3)
- The right green square (with B center) stayed at 27-29 but the white block at 30-32 is still there
Hmm, actually it looks like the green squares are moving rightward. When a green square moves, it leaves a white block behind.
The bottom bar: before it was all charcoal (c), now it's 63 c + 1 W. This could be a progress indicator.
Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if the pattern continues.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The green squares move right when RIGHT is pressed
# Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see the pattern continue
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
